_fixture_meta.description: Smart washing machine with WiFi, Bluetooth, cloud account, OTA firmware updates — complex product with multiple overlapping obligations.

request.description: Smart washing machine, 230 V mains, WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity, cloud account for remote control and usage analytics, OTA firmware updates, Energy Label A-rated, 8 kg drum

product_summary: Smart washing machine with WiFi/BT, cloud account, OTA updates (230 V, 8 kg, Energy Label A)
summary: This smart appliance spans multiple regulatory regimes. As a mains-powered household appliance it requires LVD safety testing. The WiFi and Bluetooth interfaces bring it into RED scope, superseding EMC as a separate directive. The cloud account and usage analytics trigger GDPR, and the OTA firmware update path means RED Cyber obligations (EN 18031-1/2) apply from 1 Aug 2025. The Ecodesign Regulation for washing machines (EU) 2019/2023 is mandatory. CRA (Cyber Resilience Act) will apply as a future obligation for internet-connected products. Several pieces of missing information must be resolved before the full compliance picture is complete.
analyzed_description: Smart washing machine, 230 V mains, WiFi and Bluetooth connectivity, cloud account for remote control and usage analytics, OTA firmware updates, Energy Label A-rated, 8 kg drum
normalized_description: smart washing machine 230v wifi bluetooth cloud ota firmware updates energy label a 8kg
classification_summary: Matched to washing_machine (smart_washing_machine subtype) with high confidence. WiFi/BT and cloud account confirm smart variant.
primary_uncertainties: Cloud provider data residency — may affect GDPR transfer obligations
OTA update server location — affects CRA software update requirements
Whether usage analytics include personal identifiers
route_trigger_reasons: WiFi and Bluetooth interfaces trigger RED (absorbs LVD/EMC for CE marking)
Cloud account with usage analytics triggers GDPR
OTA firmware updates trigger RED Cyber (EN 18031-1/2) obligations
Internet-connected product triggers future CRA obligations
primary_route_reason: Smart washing machine maps to EN 60335-2-7 (particular safety requirements for washing machines) as the primary safety standard. RED applies for radio interfaces; RED absorbs LVD/EMC for the CE declaration.

legislations.title: Radio Equipment Directive 2014/53/EU
legislations.reason: WiFi and Bluetooth interfaces present — RED governs safety, EMC, and radio spectrum obligations.
legislations.notes: RED absorbs LVD and EMC for CE marking purposes. A single DoC covering RED (and ROHS) is sufficient.

sub_articles.label: Network protection
sub_articles.description: Network not harmed by the device

legislations.title: RED Delegated Regulation (EU) 2022/30 — Cybersecurity obligations
legislations.reason: Internet-connected device with OTA updates — RED Art. 3.3(d/e/f) cybersecurity obligations apply from 1 Aug 2025.
legislations.notes: Mandatory from 1 August 2025. Notified body involvement may be required for Art. 3.3 assessment.

legislations.title: WEEE Directive 2012/19/EU
legislations.reason: Household electrical appliance — WEEE take-back and marking.

legislations.title: Ecodesign Regulation for washing machines and washer-dryers (EU) 2019/2023
legislations.reason: Washing machine — mandatory Ecodesign requirements for energy efficiency, water consumption, and repairability.
legislations.notes: EU Energy Label (A–G scale) mandatory. Product information sheet required.

legislations.title: General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679
legislations.reason: Cloud account with usage analytics processes personal data of identifiable users.
legislations.notes: GDPR applies to the cloud service; hardware is out of GDPR scope but product documentation must reference the privacy notice.

legislations.title: Cyber Resilience Act — Regulation (EU) 2024/2847
legislations.reason: Internet-connected product with OTA updates — CRA applies as a default important product (Annex I, Class I) from 11 Dec 2027.
legislations.notes: Reporting obligations for actively exploited vulnerabilities apply from 11 Sep 2026. Full product obligations from 11 Dec 2027.
legislations.applicability_hint: Start gap analysis now — CRA requires significant software supply chain documentation.

future_regimes.title: Cyber Resilience Act — Regulation (EU) 2024/2847
future_regimes.reason: Internet-connected product — CRA Class I from Dec 2027.
future_regimes.notes: Vulnerability reporting from Sep 2026; full obligations Dec 2027.

standards.title: Safety of household and similar electrical appliances — Part 2-7: Particular requirements for washing machines
standards.reason: Primary product-specific safety standard for washing machines. Applies under RED Art. 3.1(a).

standards.title: Safety of household and similar electrical appliances — Part 1: General requirements
EMC — emissions — household appliances
standards.reason: EMC emissions for household appliances under RED Art. 3.1(b).

standards.title: EMC — immunity — household appliances
standards.reason: EMC immunity for household appliances under RED Art. 3.1(b).

standards.title: Wideband transmission — 2.4 GHz ISM band
standards.reason: Radio performance standard for WiFi (2.4 GHz) and Bluetooth.

standards.title: EMC — common requirements for radio equipment
standards.reason: Base EMC standard for radio equipment under RED Art. 3.1(b).

standards.title: RF exposure — electronic and electrical equipment
standards.reason: RF exposure assessment required for integrated WiFi and Bluetooth.

standards.title: Common security requirements for radio equipment — Part 1: Internet connected radio equipment
standards.reason: RED Art. 3.3(d/e/f) — mandatory for internet-connected devices from 1 Aug 2025.
standards.notes: Addresses network protection (Art. 3.3(d)), personal data safeguards (Art. 3.3(e)), and fraud protection (Art. 3.3(f)).
evidence_hint: OTA update server must authenticate; verify secure boot chain

standards.title: Common security requirements for radio equipment — Part 2: Radio equipment processing personal data
standards.reason: RED Art. 3.3(e) — personal data safeguards for devices processing personal data.
standards.notes: Applies because cloud account collects usage analytics linked to identifiable users.
evidence_hint: Confirm categories of personal data collected via cloud account

review_items.title: Cyber Security for Consumer Internet of Things: Baseline Requirements
review_items.reason: Optional ETSI baseline cybersecurity standard — review whether it is referenced in the RED delegated regulation assessment.
review_items.notes: EN 18031-1/2 are the primary standards for RED Cyber; EN 303 645 may be used as supporting reference.

missing_information: Cloud provider data residency — confirm EU vs non-EU processing for GDPR Chapter V
OTA update server authentication mechanism — required for EN 18031-1 assessment
Exact categories of personal data in usage analytics
Whether product has a dedicated smart metering or energy monitoring module

missing_information_items.message: Where is the cloud service hosted? EU residency affects GDPR data transfer obligations.
next_actions: Obtain cloud provider SLA and data processing agreement
Confirm data residency with cloud engineering team

missing_information_items.message: How does the OTA update mechanism authenticate the update server and verify firmware integrity?
examples: Code signing with manufacturer private key
next_actions: Document OTA mechanism in technical file

missing_information_items.message: What personal data is collected in usage analytics? (e.g. wash programme selections linked to user ID, energy use linked to home address)
examples: Wash programme and cycle count only
Energy consumption with smart meter linkage
next_actions: Update privacy notice to cover all processing purposes

missing_information_items.message: Does the product include a dedicated smart metering or energy monitoring module with external interfaces?
examples: None — energy data via cloud only
next_actions: Confirm with hardware team whether a separate metering module is present

warnings: RED Cyber (EN 18031-1/2) obligations are mandatory from 1 Aug 2025 — verify compliance status if product is already on the market.

evidence: cloud account for remote control and usage analytics

risk_reasons.title: RED Cyber obligations mandatory from 1 Aug 2025
risk_reasons.detail: EN 18031-1 and EN 18031-2 are now mandatory. OTA update mechanism and personal data processing must be formally assessed.

risk_reasons.title: GDPR data transfer risk unresolved
risk_reasons.detail: Cloud provider data residency not confirmed. If non-EU hosting is used, SCCs or Binding Corporate Rules are required.

risk_reasons.title: CRA Class I obligations from Dec 2027
risk_reasons.detail: Cyber Resilience Act will require SBOM, vulnerability disclosure policy, and extended support commitment. Early gap analysis recommended.

items.message: Where is the cloud service hosted?
How does the OTA mechanism authenticate the update server?

next_actions: Obtain cloud provider data processing agreement
Conduct personal data mapping for analytics

top_actions: Conduct RED EMC testing per EN 55014-1/2, EN 301 489-1 + EN 301 489-17
Complete EN 18031-1 and EN 18031-2 assessment for RED Cyber obligations
Confirm cloud provider data residency and obtain GDPR DPA
Ensure Ecodesign (EU) 2019/2023 compliance and Energy Label is current
next_actions: Start CRA gap analysis — full obligations from 11 Dec 2027
Document OTA update mechanism for EN 18031-1 §6.1 compliance
Register for WEEE in target EU Member States
future_watchlist: CRA Class I obligations from 11 Dec 2027 — SBOM and VDP required
RED Cyber update obligations if EN 18031 is revised
suggested_questions: Is the cloud service hosted within the EU?
Does the OTA update use signed firmware and authenticated TLS connections?
What specific personal data fields are sent to the analytics backend?
